title: Decide whether $callers covers a Task.async call
prompt: A test contains: `task = Task.async(fn -> MyApp.Accounts.count_users() end); result = Task.await(task)`. Write `docs/task_async_analysis.md` explaining: (1) whether this test needs an explicit `Ecto.Adapters.SQL.Sandbox.allow/3` call or not, (2) why — must mention the `$callers` process dictionary tracking, (3) that allow/3 is NOT required here because Task.async is a direct child of the test process and $callers propagates. Must state the answer: allow/3 is not required.
tier_rationale: Requires knowing the $callers mechanic AND applying it to a specific scenario.
research_citation: "Spawning a task will respect the $callers/$ancestors hierarchy, and find the correct task to use" — Elixir Forum, ityonemo
